milestones.name: Cryptography (variable N)
deliverables: TS sigma protocols (Schnorr, DH-tuple, N-way sigma-OR) in offchain/src/crypto/
Aiken verifiers in contracts/lib/lovejoin/{schnorr,dhtuple,sigma_or}.ak
Rust reference impl using blst in crypto/ref/
RFC 6979 deterministic nonce derivation in offchain/src/crypto/nonce.ts
Encoding-parity test (TS hash output == Aiken hash output for 1000 random inputs)
KAT vectors in crypto/test-vectors/{provedlog,provedhtuple,sigma-or,negative}.json at N ∈ {2,3,4,6,8}
All KAT vectors verify in TS, Aiken, and Rust ref
All negative vectors are rejected by all three impls

exit_criteria.check: test -f crypto/test-vectors/provedlog.json && test -f crypto/test-vectors/provedhtuple.json && test -f crypto/test-vectors/sigma-or.json && test -f crypto/test-vectors/negative.json

node -e "const v = JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('crypto/test-vectors/sigma-or.json','utf8')); const ns = new Set(v.map(x => x.N)); for (const n of [2,3,4,6,8]) if (!ns.has(n)) { console.error('missing N=' + n); process.exit(1); }"

exit_criteria.description: RFC 6979 reproducibility: same (x, msg) yields byte-identical proof

milestones.name: Validators + Preprod bootstrap + N calibration
notes: Chain provider for v1: Blockfrost (https://blockfrost.io). Local cardano-node + ogmios + db-sync are NOT required to ship M2/M3/M4 — Blockfrost's hosted API covers tx submission + chain queries via mesh's BlockfrostProvider. The backend's ogmios + db-sync indexer (M5 spec) becomes the second implementation of the same provider interface, swappable at runtime.

Canonical test network: Preprod (via Blockfrost Preprod project ID, free at blockfrost.io). Mainnet remains gated behind the audit gate per spec OQ-Y — the bootstrap ceremony is irreversible per network and creates permanent on-chain artifacts, so do NOT run it against mainnet during pre-alpha.

Provider abstraction (offchain/src/chain/provider.ts) is introduced at M2 because the stress-test submitters need it; M3/M4/M5 inherit it. Bootstrap scripts in infra/bootstrap/ continue to use cardano-cli — they're one-shot ceremonies, not steady-state ops.

Empirical max_n calibration and the 30-minute fuzz harness ran against Preprod required a working Mix tx builder, which lives in M4. Per the user's call (2026-04-27): the M2 PR shipped validators + bootstrap + provider abstraction; the calibration + fuzz deliverables and exit criteria moved into M4 where they can actually drive a real Mix tx end-to-end.
deliverables: validators/{reference_holder,one_shot_mint,mix_box,mix_logic,fee_contract}.ak compiled successfully
mix_box is a cheap spend-side delegator; mix_logic is the withdraw-zero validator that handles Owner + Mix logic once per tx (see docs/spec/03-contracts.md §0)
Spend validators tolerate bad/missing datums (return True) per the hyperstructure principle; mix_logic silently ignores malformed mix inputs
Mix branch handles variable N at runtime (2 ≤ N)
test files cover every rule with positive + negative tests at multiple N values
contracts/build.sh produces artifacts/<network>/{*.plutus, addresses.json}
offchain/src/chain/provider.ts: ChainProvider interface (submitTx, getUtxos, awaitConfirmation, getReferenceUtxo, getProtocolParams). BlockfrostProvider implementation. Mesh wired to use it.
config/network.preprod.json gains a 'provider' block (provider: 'blockfrost', projectId env var) so swapping providers is config-only.

infra/bootstrap/00-build-reference.sh (offline data build), 01a-publish.sh (recursive tx chain via build-raw: 3 single-script-per-tx publishes), 01b-register.sh (Plutus stake-credential registration via transaction build with --certificate-tx-in-reference + --certificate-reference-tx-in-redeemer-file; run after 01a confirms), 02-mint-and-lock.sh (irreversible mint + lock at reference_holder), 03-fund-fee-contract.sh (10 fee shards). cardano-cli driven; runs on a synced node socket.
Reference NFT minted on Preprod, locked at reference_holder
10 fee shards funded on Preprod
Reference scripts published as CIP-33 references on Preprod

exit_criteria.description: ChainProvider abstraction exists and BlockfrostProvider implements it
ChainProvider unit tests pass (with a mocked HTTP layer; no live Blockfrost call required)
artifacts/preprod/addresses.json exists and has reference NFT info
exit_criteria.check: node -e "const a = JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('artifacts/preprod/addresses.json','utf8')); if (!a.referenceNftPolicy || !a.referenceUtxoRef) { console.error('missing reference NFT'); process.exit(1); }"

milestones.name: UI vertical slice — wallet + deposit + withdraw
notes: Inserted between M3 and M4 (per 2026-04-27 decision): headless integration testing of M3 is hard to debug and not how users will interact, so build the smallest possible UI that exercises the SDK with a real CIP-30 browser wallet. This validates buildDepositTx + buildWithdrawTx + the BrowserWallet adapter together, and surfaces any browser-only issues (mesh bundling, libsodium polyfill, window globals) before we go deep on M4's Mix builder.

Scope is intentionally minimal: wallet picker, deposit form, in-memory list of just-deposited boxes, withdraw form. NO IndexedDB encryption, NO i18n lint rule, NO Pool screen, NO Mix screen, NO Playwright E2E. Those land in M6 with the rest of the polish.
Build-guide §'The thin happy-path slice (target: end of M3)' prescribes this; spec just assumed CLI for the slice.
deliverables: ui/src/lib/sdk.ts — bridge that wires @lovejoin/sdk into the React app (provider config, addresses.json loading, BrowserWallet helper)
Wallet-connect button using BrowserWallet.enable; lists installed CIP-30 wallets via BrowserWallet.getInstalledWallets
Deposit screen: rounds field + Deposit button → buildDepositTx → shows tx hash + saves (a, b, x) in component state
In-memory 'My boxes' list — only the boxes deposited this session; full pool view waits for M5/M6
Withdraw screen: pick a box from the list (or paste secret + ref + a + b) + destination address → buildWithdrawTx → shows tx hash
Manual verification on Preprod: deposit + withdraw round-trip from a browser wallet; confirmation screenshots / cardanoscan links pasted into docs/m3.5-verification.md

exit_criteria.description: Verification report exists with Preprod tx links

milestones.name: Mix tx builder + Mix UI
notes: Empirical max_n calibration, the fee-calibration sweep, and the 30-minute fuzz harness moved here from M2 (per 2026-04-27 decision): they all need a real Mix tx builder to exercise. Run them after the variable-N Mix builder is functional on Preprod and commit the empirical max_n + max_fee_per_mix_lovelace into config/network.preprod.json + docs/perf.md before closing M4.

Bundles the Mix UI screen with the SDK work (per 2026-04-27 decision): same reasoning as M3.5 — verifying Mix headlessly is harder than just building the UI to drive it. Pool view (which requires backend chain-scan) stays deferred until M5/M6.

offchain/src/pool/{identify,select}.ts pool scanner and uniform random N-tuple selector
lovejoin CLI: mix subcommand with --n and --rounds
ui Mix screen: pick boxes from the M3.5 'my boxes' list (or paste explicit refs), choose N, run mix, show tx hash. Pool-wide random selection is M5/M6.
Integration tests: 30 mixes at varied N, full lifecycle, fee-shard exhaustion behavior, collateral provider unreachable behavior
All integration tests pass 10 consecutive runs
stress-tests/max-n-calibration.ts and stress-tests/fee-calibration.ts run against Preprod via Blockfrost and update network.preprod.json (deferred from M2)
docs/perf.md updated with empirical max_n, max_fee_per_mix_lovelace, and Mix tx CPU at max_n (deferred from M2)
30-minute fuzz run completes with no panics or unexpected accepts (deferred from M2)

exit_criteria.description: Mix tx builder unit tests pass

exit_criteria.check: node -e "const c = JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('config/network.preprod.json','utf8')); if (!c.max_n || c.max_n < 2) { console.error('max_n not set or invalid'); process.exit(1); }"

exit_criteria.description: Mix tx CPU at max_n is under 70% of mainnet limit

milestones.name: Mix tx CPU/mem optimization
notes: Inserted between M4 and M5 (per 2026-04-27 decision after live Preprod testing): N=4 overshoots the per-tx CPU budget (10G mainnet steps) on the validator we shipped with M4. The Mix tx's per-tx cost is dominated by ~2N² bls12_381_g1_uncompress calls plus ~4N² scalar muls; bringing the constant down (rather than capping max_n at 3) is the highest-leverage path to making the protocol useful for real users. Privacy at width N=3 reaches 1/2^30 linkage in 19 rounds vs 12 at N=4 — a ~60% rise in user fees.

First optimisation already shipped on the M4 branch: 'perf(m4): pre-uncompress sigma-OR statements in mix_logic' — drops N² statement-point uncompresses to N. M4.5's job is the rest of the cost-cutting + a redeploy + re-calibration.
deliverables: Audit + optimise validators/{mix_logic,sigma_or,fee_contract}.ak for redundant uncompresses, redundant hashes, redundant traversals, and unnecessary intermediate list allocations. The cost model is roughly: bls12_381_g1_uncompress ≈ 25-30M CPU > scalar_mul ≈ 10M > blake2b_256 ≈ 4M for typical Mix preimages > add/equal ≈ 1M.

Ship the new validators via a fresh bootstrap on Preprod (irreversible; old fee shards + boxes are orphaned). Bump max_fee_per_mix_lovelace in the new ReferenceDatum to leave headroom over the empirical post-optimisation fee.
Re-run stress-tests/max-n-calibration.ts on the new deployment; record the new max_n + the per-N CPU/mem table in docs/perf.md.
Re-run the M4 integration test suite (mix-n2, mix-at-max-n, fee-exhaustion, full-lifecycle) on the new deployment; update artifacts/preprod/addresses.json.
Document the optimisation choices made + the ones rejected (and why) in docs/perf.md so future contributors can pick up where we left off.

exit_criteria.description: All SDK unit tests still pass

exit_criteria.check: node -e "const c = JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('config/network.preprod.json','utf8')); if (!c.max_n || c.max_n < 4) { console.error('max_n not >= 4 (is ' + c.max_n + ')'); process.exit(1); }"

exit_criteria.description: Per-N exec-units table recorded in docs/perf.md
Preprod integration test mix-at-max-n passes against the new deployment

milestones.name: Backend indexer + API
notes: Execution-ordering decision (2026-04-27): deferred until after M4 + M6 polish. The M2 ChainProvider abstraction means Blockfrost is sufficient for the alpha — every SDK + UI call goes through it. The self-hosted ogmios + db-sync indexer is a second ChainProvider implementation, swap-in via config when Blockfrost rate limits or self-hosting needs hit. Pool-view UI in M6 reads from whichever provider is wired.
deliverables: backend/src/indexer/{ogmios,pool,fee,reference,reorg}.ts
backend/src/api/ with all routes from 05-backend.md
backend/Dockerfile
Load test demonstrates p99 < 100ms for /pool with 50k boxes
Recovery test demonstrates 500-block rollback recovery
Reference-UTxO sanity alarm test

M6 now finishes the rest — Pool/Vault/Box/Home screens, IndexedDB encrypted box-secret storage (Argon2id), i18n lint, the N-width slider, Seedelf destination detection, Playwright E2E. Pool view depends on M5's indexer being wired (or Blockfrost-direct as a fallback).
deliverables: Remaining screens from 06-ui.md (Home, Pool, Vault, Box) — Deposit + Withdraw shipped in M3.5; Mix shipped in M4
IndexedDB encrypted storage for box secrets (Argon2id passphrase) — replaces M3.5's in-memory state
i18n English at minimum; lint rule against raw English in JSX
N-width slider on Pool screen
Collateral provider status indicator + disabled-mix-button when down
Seedelf destination detection on Withdraw screen
E2E Playwright test on Preprod

exit_criteria.description: E2E Playwright test passes on Preprod

milestones.name: UI v1.5 — design pass + UX rework
notes: Inserted between M6 and M7 (per 2026-04-27 dogfood review): M6 shipped every spec'd screen + behavior, but the result reads as a test page. M6.5 is the design + UX pass that turns it into something a real user can use without prior protocol knowledge.

Owner-finds-utxo refactor (deliverable 5) supersedes the M3.5/M4 'in-memory boxes' contract: with [x]·a == b, the SDK can already locate every owned box from just the secret via findOwnedBoxes(). The vault should store secrets only — current ref + (a, b) come from a fresh pool scan on every unlock. Stored a/b/ref values go stale the moment a box is mixed.

Calibrated max_n on Preprod is 3 (per docs/perf.md / live testing); the M6 slider's hard-coded 6 is wrong. M4.5's optimisation pass aims to lift it back to 4+ but until that lands the UI must reflect the deployed reality.
deliverables: Visual design pass: real splash on Home (not a status-dump dashboard); brand-aware header with the connect-wallet CTA promoted to the top bar; consistent typography + spacing + color tokens; loading / empty / error states for every screen; Configuration panel moved to a Settings route (or modal) so the front page isn't a debug surface.
Wallet UX: connect/disconnect lives in the header (visible state + change-address truncated to one line); installed-wallet picker rendered as a modal on click; never as a floating section panel.

Vault UX rework: opt-in encrypted-file backup on first vault create (download .lovejoin-vault.json); passphrase strength meter + confirm-passphrase on create; recovery copy that explains the 'no recovery path' tradeoff before reset; lock-on-inactivity timer.

Pool slider clamps to the empirical max_n (currently 3, sourced from network.<net>.json or the addresses bundle — surface it to the UI rather than hard-coding); inline copy explains the privacy-vs-cost tradeoff per N.
Fee-payer toggle on the Pool MixButton (wallet vs shard) — the SDK already supports it; the M6 Mix flow hard-codes 'shard'. Restore the choice with a clear explanation of the anonymity tradeoff.

Owner-finds-utxo refactor: vault stores ONLY the owner secret (32-byte scalar) + label; on unlock, the Vault screen scans the pool via findOwnedBoxes() and renders the live (ref, a, b). Box detail + Withdraw read from the live scan, not stale stored datums. Removes the manual paste-the-mix-box-bytes affordance from Withdraw entirely (it's an advanced disclosure at most).

Withdraw UX: vault-backed primary flow (pick a stored box + destination); address book / paste-history affordance; one-click 'create a Seedelf destination' as a first-class option (delegates to Seedelf when present); manual hex paste hidden behind an 'advanced' disclosure.

Privacy-UX rules audit (06-ui.md §Privacy UX rules 1–8): explicit fee/collateral source line on every Mix tx preview; banner copy revisions; double-check no analytics/cookies/Buffer-tracking sneaks in via mesh transitive deps.

exit_criteria.description: Pool slider max equals network.preprod.json max_n at runtime (not hard-coded)
exit_criteria.check: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('config/network.preprod.json','utf8')); const t=fs.readFileSync('ui/src/routes/Pool.tsx','utf8'); if (/DEFAULT_MAX_N\s*=\s*[0-9]/.test(t)) { console.error('Pool.tsx still has a hard-coded DEFAULT_MAX_N'); process.exit(1); } if (!t.includes('max_n') && !t.includes('maxN')) { console.error('Pool.tsx does not reference dynamic max_n'); process.exit(1); }"

exit_criteria.description: Vault stores secrets only (no aHex/bHex in the schema)
exit_criteria.check: node -e "const t=require('fs').readFileSync('ui/src/storage/secrets.ts','utf8'); if (/aHex\s*:\s*string/.test(t) || /bHex\s*:\s*string/.test(t)) { console.error('secrets.ts still persists aHex/bHex on StoredBox'); process.exit(1); }"

exit_criteria.description: Pool MixButton exposes the fee-payer toggle
